records.ety: [L. structura, from struere, structum, to arrange, build, construct; perhaps akin to E. strew: cf. F. structure. Cf. Construe, Destroy, Instrument, Obstruct.]

defs: The act of building; the practice of erecting buildings; construction.
quotes: His son builds on, and never is content Till the last farthing is in structure spent.

defs: Manner of building; form; make; construction.
quotes: Want of insight into the structure and constitution of the terraqueous globe.

defs: Arrangement of parts, of organs, or of constituent particles, in a substance or body; as, the structure of a rock or a mineral; the structure of a sentence.
quotes: It [basalt] has often a prismatic structure.

defs: Manner of organization; the arrangement of the different tissues or parts of animal and vegetable organisms; as, organic structure, or the structure of animals and plants; cellular structure.
That which is built; a building; esp., a building of some size or magnificence; an edifice.
quotes: There stands a structure of majestic frame.
